evals.prompt: Build a 10-year financial model for a 50MW hyperscale data center with 70% senior debt at 6% interest and 30% equity. Total CapEx is $500M. Revenue is contracted at $140/kW/month with 100% occupancy from Year 1 (take-or-pay). OpEx is 50% of revenue. Model MACRS 5-year accelerated depreciation plus 30% ITC on $50M of qualifying solar CapEx. Equity hurdle rate is 10%. Discount rate 8%.
evals.expected_output: A financial model showing: $350M senior debt with amortization schedule, $150M equity, contracted revenue stream with no occupancy risk, EBITDA margins around 50%, levered equity IRR in the 10-15% range, MOIC around 1.5-2.0x over 10 years, DSCR above 1.25x in all years, MACRS depreciation benefit, ITC of $15M (30% of $50M), equity waterfall with preferred return and carried interest distribution, and sensitivity analysis.
assertions: IRR is calculated for both project (unlevered) and equity (levered)
NPV is calculated at 8% discount rate
MOIC is computed as total distributions divided by equity invested
Multi-tranche waterfall includes preferred return at 10% hurdle
MACRS 5-year depreciation schedule is applied to qualifying equipment
ITC of 30% on $50M qualifying solar CapEx produces $15M credit
DSCR is calculated and maintained above covenant minimum
Financial and Tax disclaimers are included in output

evals.prompt: Create a 15-year financial model for a 20MW colocation facility with 3-year occupancy ramp (30% Year 1, 60% Year 2, 85% Year 3, 95% stabilized). Revenue at $150/kW/month retail pricing. Capital structure: $120M total CapEx, 65% senior debt at 5.5%, 10% mezzanine at 13% coupon, 25% equity. Mezzanine has 2-year interest-only period. Equity hurdle 12%, 80/20 LP/GP split with catch-up. Include 100% bonus depreciation post-OBBBA.
evals.expected_output: A 15-year financial model with: 3-tranche capital stack ($78M senior, $12M mezz, $30M equity), occupancy ramp impacting early-year revenue, mezzanine IO period modeled, EBITDA growing with occupancy, levered equity IRR reflecting occupancy ramp risk, MOIC target of 2.0-2.5x, equity waterfall with preferred return, GP catch-up, and carried interest split, bonus depreciation tax benefit in Year 1.
assertions: Occupancy ramp is modeled: 30%, 60%, 85%, then 95% stabilized
Mezzanine debt is modeled with 2-year interest-only period
Equity waterfall has preferred return, catch-up, and carried interest
Bonus depreciation at 100% is applied in Year 1 post-OBBBA
MOIC is calculated reflecting multi-year investment horizon
Sensitivity analysis includes occupancy rate as a key variable
Financial and Tax disclaimers are included with 'not investment advice' text
Revenue escalation is applied after stabilization

evals.prompt: Model a 100MW sovereign data center with government cost-plus contract. Total CapEx $1.5B at $15M/MW (sovereign premium). Revenue is cost-plus-10% margin guaranteed by government appropriation. Capital structure: 20% government debt at 3% (subsidized) and 80% government equity (no external equity). Projection period 20 years. No ITC or PTC. MACRS applies to qualifying equipment. Discount rate 5% (government WACC). No equity waterfall needed (single government entity).
evals.expected_output: A 20-year financial model showing: minimal leverage, cost-plus revenue with guaranteed margin, stable EBITDA at 10% of costs, very low levered returns (5-8% matching government cost of capital), high NPV due to low discount rate, no equity waterfall complexity, MACRS depreciation on qualifying equipment, and sensitivity analysis showing construction cost as primary variable.
assertions: Cost-plus revenue model produces stable 10% margin above OpEx
Low leverage (20% debt) reflects government financing structure
20-year projection captures full asset lifecycle
IRR reflects low-risk government-backed structure
NPV is calculated at 5% government WACC discount rate
MACRS depreciation is applied despite government ownership
Financial disclaimer is included noting estimates only
Sensitivity analysis identifies construction cost as top driver
